I don't agree with J.K. on feminism at all but I absolutely respect her for standing up for her values and morals in an opposing environment, place and era. She's one of the women who actually would've fought against the grain back when feminism was a movement for positive and logical change, as opposed to all the band-wagoners who act as though they're standing up to "the establishment" by parroting every stance and statement of said "establishment", in an environment where doing so gets you padded on the back and privileged, rather than shouted at, fired and sometimes even physically harmed. J.K. is a woman of her convictions and that deserves respect.

Disclaimer: I don't think the mere act of standing up for one's convictions is inherently respectable. If one's conviction is that everyone with freckles should be enslaved for example, I'd find them standing up for it and refusing to back down a testament to their immorality, not the fortitude of their character. However, I don't find J.K.'s feministic presmises or convictions immoral for the most part but rather misguided. Only semi-immoral in the same way as it would've been semi-immoral in 17th century north America to form a movement for the advancement of white people's rights. Another thing to notice is the "Pah" sound (Like the sound at the beginning of the words "planet" and "person"). You will notice that "pah" sounds exactly the same at the beginning of every pah-sounding word. When we humans speak, we never sound in the exact same tone. There is always some slight variation.
